aliases: Tanzim Qaidat al-Jihad fi Bilad al-Rafidayn
description: Salafi jihadist terrorist organization affiliated with al-Qaeda, founded on 17 October 2004 by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi. It operated in Iraq, targeting U.S.-led coalition forces, Iraqi Shiites, and others. The group disbanded on 15 October 2006 to form the Islamic State of Iraq.

country_links.evidence_quote: 6 July: AQI claimed responsibility for the kidnapping and execution of Egypt 's ambassador to Iraq, Ihab el-Sherif.
country_links.context: AQI kidnapped and executed Egypt's ambassador to Iraq, Ihab el-Sherif.

country_links.evidence_quote: they claimed responsibility for the rocket attacks which ... also targeted the city of Eilat in Israel
country_links.context: AQI claimed responsibility for rocket attacks targeting the city of Eilat in Israel.

country_links.evidence_quote: At the end of October 2004, al-Qaeda in Iraq kidnapped Japanese citizen Shosei Koda.
country_links.context: AQI kidnapped Japanese citizen Shosei Koda and demanded Japan withdraw troops from Iraq, then beheaded him.

country_links.evidence_quote: 19 August: In the Jordanian city of Aqaba , a rocket attack kills a Jordanian soldier.
country_links.context: AQI carried out a rocket attack in Aqaba killing a Jordanian soldier, and bombed three hotels in Amman killing 60 people.

country_links.evidence_quote: for the firing of several rockets into Israel from Lebanon in December 2005.
country_links.context: AQI claimed responsibility for firing rockets into Israel from Lebanon.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 3 June 2006, AQI abducted and killed four Russian diplomats in Iraq .
country_links.context: AQI abducted and killed four Russian diplomats in Iraq.
